generated_by: scheduled-task workday-t10-marketing-subject-lines — assistant fallback (marketing-skills bridge unavailable from daemon shell: no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, no plugin root on disk)

input.topic: End-of-day customer update on a day where the build is on or ahead of plan. Names what shipped, what's next, links preview + build log.
input.scenario: Sent at 5pm ET by the build agent on a 'good day' (planned-vs-shipped delta within one ticket). Customer reads in the evening, wants the receipt without ceremony.
input.voice: Operator. 5pm, 37 words, no fluff. Signed '— Jack / Day14'.

variants.subject: Day {{n}} — {{one_thing_shipped_short}}
variants.rationale: Keep the current line. Day-count is the calendar anchor; the shipped-thing token guarantees the subject is uniquely informative every day.

variants.subject: Day {{n}} update — shipped {{thing_1}}
variants.rationale: Adds the verb 'shipped' so the customer sees forward motion in the subject. Still mobile-safe with reasonable {{thing_1}} values.

variants.subject: EOD {{n}}: on track for {{launch_date}}
variants.rationale: Skips per-day detail in favor of the trust signal customers care about most — am I going to launch on time. Useful for the final third of the build.
